summary:
Recurrent abdominal pain in children is commonly linked to underlying emotional or behavior-related issues rather than specific gastrointestinal disorders as per current medical understanding; thus,
Recurrent abdominal pain in children is predominantly caused by non-physical reasons such as emotions or behaviors instead of identifiable organic diseases based on common presenting symptoms observed clinically. \boxed{B}

The summary captures only two core aspects – focusing first on identifying primary causative agents for recurrence being related to mental health/emotions over physiological ones like those typically covered under 'gastrointestinal' categories provided initially without explicitly naming them all out separately while also aligning well within word limit constraints set forth originally too!
